audit_items.proof_trace: TxPool and BlockValidator share the same TxValidator singleton that enforces Eip7825Constants.DefaultTxGasLimitCap (16,777,216). However, TxPool uses GetCurrentHeadSpec() while BlockValidator uses GetSpec(block.Header), creating a race window during fork activation where TxPool may accept transactions under the old spec that BlockValidator rejects under the new spec.
audit_items.attack_scenario: During EIP-7825 fork activation at block N, submit transaction with gasLimit=20M. TxPool accepts it (old spec, cap=MaxValue), peers broadcast it, but block producers reject it when building block N (new spec, cap=16,777,216). This creates mempool pollution and wastes network bandwidth on un-mineable transactions.
